author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
śrīvāsa⋅vacana, śravaṇa kariyā,
sādhvī pativratāgaṇa
śoka parihari’, mṛta śiśu rākhi’,
hari⋅rase dila mana

śrīvāsa takhana, ānande mātiyā,
aṅgane āila punaḥ
nāce gorā⋅sane, sakala pāsari,
gāya nandasuta⋅guṇa

cāri daṇḍa rātre, marila kumāra,
aṅgane keha nā jāne
śrī⋅nāma⋅maṅgale, tṛtīya prahara,
rajanī atīta gāne

kīrtana bhāṅgile, kahe gaura⋅hari,
āji kena pāi duḥkha ?
bujhi, ei gṛhe, kichu amaṅgala,
ghaṭiyā harila sukha

tabe bhakta-jana, nivedana kare,
śrīvāsa⋅śiśura kathā
śuni’ gorārāya, bale, hāya hāya,
marame pāinu vyathā

keha nā kahile, āmāre takhana,
vipada⋅saṁvāda sabe
bhakativinoda, bhakata⋅vatsala,
snehete majila tabe
en_translation:
Hearing Śrīvāsa's words, the chaste and devoted wives gave up their grief; leaving the dead child, they gave their minds to the nectar of the Lord's service.

Śrīvāsa then, intoxicated with bliss, came back to the courtyard. He danced with Lord Caitanya, forgetting everything, and sang the glories of the son of Nanda.

Four units of time into the night, the boy died, but no one in the courtyard knew. The late night passed in the auspicious chanting of the Holy Name.

When the congregational chanting ended, Lord Caitanya said, "Why do I feel sorrow today? I understand that some inauspiciousness has occurred in this house, taking away the happiness."

Then the devotees related the story of Śrīvāsa's child. Hearing this, the Lord said, "Alas, alas! I feel pain in My heart!

"Why did no one tell Me the news of this calamity then?" Bhaktivinoda says: the Lord, who is affectionate to His devotees, was then immersed in love.
